description: VideoCore General Command (vcgencmd) acts as the bridge to the Raspberry Pi's core GPU processing unit, offering absolute unadulterated access to voltages, system clocks, and hardware throttling states.

commands.explanation: Extracts the exact real-time celsius temperature directly from the central SoC silicon.
Outputs the current operational CPU processing frequency in Hertz. Crucial for verifying if an overclock is adhering or if throttling is occurring.
Measures the operational frequency of the GPU core, distinct from the ARM CPU cores.
Identifies the core electrical voltage being inherently consumed. Overvoltage limits generally halt around 1.4v dynamically.
The ultimate hardware health check. Returns a 20-bit hexadecimal string indicating if under-voltage has occurred or if current thermal throttling is actively engaged.
Examines the system's memory split showing exactly how many Megabytes of ram are strictly designated to the CPU.
Outputs the specific block of Megabytes explicitly reserved for graphical rendering operations.
Returns '1' if the HDMI display connection is fundamentally energized, and '0' if it is asleep.
Dumps the absolute matrix of all numerical system configurations currently cached and operational from the /boot/config.txt.
Verifies whether the hardware-accelerated H264 video decoding block is fundamentally licensed and engaged natively on the SoC.
Extracts the absolutely explicit maximum localized Celsius boundary natively fundamentally establishing hardware-level unmitigated SoC throttling limits dynamically.
Checks massively dense explicit core relocation memory mapping inherently identifying strictly defined graphics fragmentation processing dynamically.
Dumps the explicit deep active internal hardware limits identifying native exactly bridged optical resolution arrays connected internally to the raw native ribbon bindings.
Triggers highly volatile unencrypted silicon parsing actively inherently dumping native inherently permanent One-Time-Programmable silicon register strings uniquely identifying identical boards.
Exclusively monitors explicit localized heavily distinct hardware block controllers dynamically outputting the active precise integer Hertz speed powering native video algorithms.
